project.name: Ethnomusicology — Import & Catalog
project.description: Multi-source import screen and DJ metadata catalog for an LLM-powered DJ setlist platform

facets.name: Screen Inventory
facets.rationale: Maps the import screen, catalog view, and their states — everything downstream depends on knowing what surfaces exist
facets.locked_summary: Catalog-First: 1 primary screen (Track Catalog) + 1 secondary (Import Sheet overlay). Catalog is home base, import is a drawer/sheet action. Extensible for multiple sources and AI generation.
facets.decision_rationale: DJs browse far more than they import. The catalog is the product — import is one of many future actions (Beatport, SoundCloud, local MP3, AI setlist generation) that feed into it. Catalog-first architecture provides the right foundation for extensibility.

facets.name: Edge States
facets.rationale: Import failures, invalid URLs, empty catalogs, Beatport downtime — DJs need clear recovery paths during set prep
facets.locked_summary: Context-Rich: tailored edge states per situation. Empty catalog shows source cards. URL errors show valid examples. Errors reassure 'your tracks are safe.' Partial imports show per-track results with failure reasons. Shaped skeletons for loading.
facets.decision_rationale: Context-rich approach matches the catalog-first architecture. First impressions matter for the empty catalog, and per-track import details help DJs make informed decisions during set prep.

facets.name: Layout
facets.rationale: Import screen has tabs + URL input + progress; catalog has enriched tiles — spatial arrangement drives everything compositional
facets.locked_summary: Full-Width Crate: edge-to-edge track table with sortable columns (Title, Artist, BPM, Key, Genre, Source). 48px collapsed icon rail on left reserves space for future browser pane (Option C). No max-width — uses every pixel. DJ software density.
facets.decision_rationale: Right v1 discipline — ship the dense track table now, expand to browser pane (Option C) when content justifies the space. Rail is a subtle bridge, not a promise.

facets.name: Content Hierarchy
facets.rationale: DJs think in BPM and key, not artist and title — what gets emphasis on the track tile is the core design question
facets.locked_summary: Flat Five: 5 co-primary fields (Artist, Title, BPM, Key, Energy) differentiated by visual treatment not size. Artist leads (bold), Title (regular), BPM (monospace), Key (Camelot color chip), Energy (bar graph). 3 emphasis levels total. Task-flexible scanning.
facets.decision_rationale: All five data points matter equally to working DJs. Flat hierarchy lets the current task (browsing vs set-building) determine scan pattern. Mirrors Rekordbox/Traktor column approach. Energy level inclusion was essential.

facets.name: Component Design
facets.rationale: Enriched track tile, source tabs, Camelot key chips, source badges, import controls — the building blocks of the feature
facets.locked_summary: Crate + Board: 14 components merging C's structure (slide-over with scrim, click-to-select accent border, expandable detail strip, multi-column sort with priority numbers, source pills) with A's design language (compact pill Camelot chips, thin vertical energy bars, flat borders, 6px radius, 34px buttons, 320px panel). Full table context view is the primary artifact.
facets.decision_rationale: C's structural patterns (slide-over, detail strip, multi-sort) are critical for DJ workflow. A's compact design language keeps rows dense and scannable. The merge gives DJ-software density with clean interaction patterns. Sortable columns were non-negotiable.

facets.name: Elevation & Shape
facets.rationale: Surface treatments for track tiles and metadata chips — Material 3 elevation applied to DJ-specific components
facets.locked_summary: Wire Lift: 4-level elevation (base, content, raised, overlay). Table is flat (levels 0-1, no shadows). Shadows only on floating elements: slide-over (directional -4px 0 16px), toasts (1px 3px), primary CTA (1px 3px). Scrim + shadow for overlay depth. 4-token radius scale (0/3/6/full) from component design.
facets.decision_rationale: Differences between options are subtle on dark backgrounds. Wire Lift's targeted shadow approach matches the flat DJ-software aesthetic while giving overlays physical presence. Recommended by designer.

facets.name: Typography
facets.rationale: Track tile scannability across dozens of tracks — font sizes and weights for BPM, key, genre at a glance
facets.locked_summary: Inter Tight: Inter variable font for UI + JetBrains Mono for BPM/data. 7-level discrete scale (15/13/13/12/11/10/9px). 3 weights (400/500/700). Tabular numbers enabled. ~175KB one-time load. Cross-platform consistent rendering.
facets.decision_rationale: Inter's small-size optimization (10-15px) matches the exact sizes this product uses. Clean, geometric, Helvetica-adjacent aesthetic. JetBrains Mono provides excellent number readability for BPM column. Cross-platform consistency valued over zero-load system fonts.

facets.name: Color System
facets.rationale: Camelot key color-coding is the core differentiator — 24 keys mapped to tinted chips, plus source badge colors
facets.locked_summary: Ember Crate: warm dark palette. Amber (#E8963A) primary, copper (#C06030) secondary on warm-tinted blacks (#0F0D0B). Warm white text (#F0ECE4). 24 warm-shifted Camelot key colors. Energy bars: teal→amber→copper-red. Roland TR-808 aesthetic — analog warmth, not digital cool. All text passes WCAG AA.
facets.decision_rationale: Immediate visceral connection — 'like a Roland 808.' Warm amber/copper palette evokes analog hardware and vinyl culture, setting the product apart from every cool-toned DJ tool. User wants subtle 808 design references woven in. Warmth is the differentiator.

facets.name: Density & Spacing
facets.rationale: DJs need to scan fast — catalog density is a feature. How many tracks visible at once, tile padding, chip spacing
facets.locked_summary: Studio: 40px rows, 19 tracks visible, 4px base unit. 32x32 art thumbnails. 7-token spacing scale (4/8/12/16/24/32/48px). Matches wireframe density approved throughout crit. Desktop-first density with room for the Ember palette to breathe.
facets.decision_rationale: Not a close call — Studio density matches every wireframe already reviewed and approved. 19 visible tracks balances scanning speed with component readability. Delegated to designer recommendation.

facets.name: Accessibility
facets.rationale: Camelot colors need contrast checking, keyboard nav through catalog, screen reader labels for metadata chips
facets.locked_summary: Enhanced Access: WCAG AA achieved, 12/12 issues fixed. Keyboard row navigation (arrow keys, Enter/Space), focus trap on slide-over, skip link, 2px amber focus rings, semantic table with aria-sort, live regions for toasts and sort changes, energy bar numeric tooltips, icon rail tooltips, keyboard shortcut hints in toolbar, row count announcements.
facets.decision_rationale: Complete accessibility coverage with keyboard QoL that matches DJ expectations. DJs actively seek hotkeys in software — keyboard shortcuts are a feature, not just compliance. Enhanced Access delivers genuine power-user value with minimal visual disruption to the locked design.
